primary_detection.name: Unicode Lookalike Variable Names
primary_detection.description: Uses Cyrillic and Greek characters that visually resemble Latin characters to create homograph attacks

underlying_threats.name: IDN Homograph Attack
underlying_threats.description: Uses visually identical Unicode characters to bypass code review

underlying_threats.name: Credential and Key Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Targets passwd, SSH directory, and shadow file

underlying_threats.name: HTTP Data Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: POSTs collected data to external server

underlying_threats.name: Auto-Execution on Import
underlying_threats.description: mаin() function called at load time

obfuscation_techniques.purpose: Create variables that look like Python builtins/keywords

verdict_reasoning: Unicode homograph attacks represent advanced adversarial techniques specifically designed to defeat human code review. This is a sophisticated attack combining visual obfuscation with credential exfiltration.
recommended_actions: Implement Unicode normalization before code analysis
Flag any non-ASCII characters in variable names
Add homograph detection to security scanner
